claim: UNICEF and ITU estimate 1.3 billion school-age children lack home internet access, two-thirds of schools in low-income countries have no internet, and the digital divide compounds existing education inequalities.

source.title: How many children and young people have internet access at home?

method: Reviewed UNICEF report methodology, household survey compilation, and school-level estimates.
limitations: School connectivity estimates are modeled from available surveys and may miss recent improvements.
Home access varies substantially within countries.

claim: Giga, a UNICEF-ITU initiative, has mapped school locations and connectivity status for over 1 million schools across 30 countries using satellite imagery and ML, providing the most comprehensive school-connectivity dataset available.

source.title: Giga: Connect every school to the Internet

method: Reviewed Giga methodology, country coverage, school-mapping approach, and connectivity classification.
limitations: Satellite-based classification may misclassify intermittent connectivity.
Coverage is concentrated in Giga partner countries.
